author: e-ddykim
title: Python unit tests artifact upload fails with ENOTFOUND on self-hosted Windows runner
signature: Failed to CreateArtifact: Unable to make request: ENOTFOUND|Network

failed_jobs.name: Python unit tests
failed_jobs.symptom: All 81 tests passed but artifact upload step (Upload Test Results) failed with ENOTFOUND

key_errors:
##[error]Failed to CreateArtifact: Unable to make request: ENOTFOUND
If you are using self-hosted runners, please make sure your runner has access to all GitHub endpoints
root_cause: The self-hosted Windows runner successfully ran all Python unit tests (81 passed, 4 skipped) but failed at the final 'Upload Test Results' step (Step 17) when trying to reach GitHub's artifact storage API. The error 'ENOTFOUND' indicates a DNS resolution failure or network connectivity loss to GitHub's API endpoints at that moment. This is a transient network interruption on the runner side and is completely unrelated to the GPU dynamic shape fix in PR #36738.
reproduction_steps: N/A - transient network failure not reliably reproducible
recommended_actions: Re-run only the failed jobs to retry the artifact upload step
Monitor Windows self-hosted runner network stability
If ENOTFOUND errors recur, investigate runner DNS configuration and GitHub endpoint connectivity
prevention_strategies: Ensure self-hosted Windows runners maintain stable DNS resolution to GitHub endpoints. Consider adding retry logic or making artifact upload steps non-blocking for overall job success.
historical_context: No prior occurrences of this exact ENOTFOUND artifact upload failure. The repo-memory shows a similar pattern: 'Windows Build openvino_package upload fails silently before blob upload on self-hosted runner' (Infrastructure category) from 2026-07-01, which is a different failure mode but also a Windows self-hosted runner network/upload issue.
